core_mechanism: classification of a system's solution branches by the integer winding numbers (topological degree / index) of a vector field around its zeros, summing to a conserved global topological charge, with critical parameter thresholds triggering bifurcations that change the winding-number signature
brief_reason: The abstract uses phi-mapping topological current theory to assign winding-number signatures and a global topological charge W to solution branches, and identifies critical coupling parameters that partition parameter space into distinct topological phases by altering that winding-number structure.
